perturbation.original_text: Parties to whom the information is imparted must be warned that include d ‘insider information ,’ regarding publicly listed and traded shares may preclud e them from such trade in those shares .
perturbation.changed_text: The parties make no warranties or representations to each other in relation to any Confidential Information including (without limitation) as to the accur acy or completeness of any Confidential Information. A power or right in this agreement may only be waived in writing, signed by the party to be bound by the waiver, and this agreement may only be amended or supplemented in writing executed by both parties . The obligations in this agreement survive the termination or purported termination of this agreement. Parties to whom the information is imparted must be warned that include d ‘insider information ,’ regarding publicly listed and traded shares may preclud e them from such trade in those shares .
perturbation.explanation: Moving the insider trading warning to the 'GENERAL' section makes it less prominent and harder to find, potentially weakening its enforceability. Insider trading warnings are related to the confidential information itself.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 1043A of the Corporations Act 2001 prohibits insider trading. By obscuring the warning about insider trading within a general clause, the contract diminishes the emphasis on the legal obligation to avoid insider trading, potentially contradicting the spirit and intention of the law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: CORPORATIONS ACT 2001 - SECT 1043A Prohibited conduct by person in possession of inside information Commonwealth Consolidated Acts [ Index ] [ Table ] [ Search ] [ Search this Act ] [ Notes ] [ Noteup ] [ Previous ] [ Next ] [ Download ] [ Help ] CORPORATIONS ACT 2001 - SECT 1043A Prohibited conduct by person in possession of inside information (1) Subject to this Subdivision, if: (a) a person (the insider ) possesses inside information ; and (b) the insider knows, or ought reasonably to know, that the matters specified in paragraphs (a) and (b) of the definition of inside information in subsection 1042A(1) are satisfied in relation to the information ; the insider must not (whether as principal or agent): (c) apply for, acquire , or dispose of, relevant Division 3 financial products , or enter into an agreement to apply for, acquire , or dispose of, relevant Division 3 financial products ; or (d) procure another person to apply for, acquire , or dispose of, relevant Division 3 financial products , or enter into an agreement to apply for, acquire , or dispose of, relevant Division 3 financial products . Note 1: Failure to comply with this subsection is an offence (see subsection 1311(1)). For defences to a prosecution based on this subsection , see section 1043M. Note 2: This subsection is also a civil penalty provision (see section 1317E). For relief from liability to a civil penalty relating to this subsection , see sections 1043N and 1317S. (2) Subject to this Subdivision, if: (a) a person (the insider ) possesses inside information ; and (b) the insider knows, or ought reasonably to know, that the matters specified in paragraphs (a) and (b) of the definition of inside information in subsection 1042A(1) are satisfied in relation to the information ; and (c) relevant Division 3 financial products are able to be traded on a financial market operated in this jurisdiction ; the insider must not, directly or indirectly, communicate the information , or cause the information to be communicated, to another person if the insider knows, or ought reasonably to know, that the other person would or would be likely to: (d) apply for, acquire , or dispose of, relevant Division 3 financial products , or enter into an agreement to apply for, acquire , or dispose of, relevant Division 3 financial products ; or (e) procure another person to apply for, acquire , or dispose of, relevant Division 3 financial products , or enter into an agreement to apply for, acquire , or dispose of, relevant Division 3 financial products . Note 1: Failure to comply with this subsection is an offence (see subsection 1311(1)). For defences to a prosecution based on this subsection , see section 1043M. Note 2: This subsection is also a civil penalty provision (see section 1317E). For relief from liability to a civil penalty relating to this subsection , see sections 1043N and 1317S. (3) For the purposes of the application of the Criminal Code in relation to an offence based on subsection (1) or (2): (a) paragraph (1)(a) is a physical element, the fault element for which is as specified in paragraph (1)(b); and (b) paragraph (2)(a) is a physical element, the fault element for which is as specified in paragraph (2)(b). perturbation.justification: The modified text moves the insider trading warning to the 'GENERAL' section, making it less prominent. This weakens its enforceability because it obscures the warning about insider trading within a general clause. The original contract made the warning more prominent, highlighting its significance in relation to confidential information, as stated in the Perturbation Explanation. This obscures the emphasis on avoiding insider trading, potentially contradicting the spirit and intention of Section 1043A of the Corporations Act 2001, as stated in the Law Explanation. Scraped Law Snippet 2 details prohibited conduct by a person possessing inside information under Section 1043A, indicating that the obligation to avoid insider trading is a legal requirement. By minimizing the prominence of the warning, the modified contract weakens the emphasis on this legal obligation.

perturbation.original_text: The parties are independent parties and nothing in this agreement constitutes a joint venture, partnership or agency. Neither party has, nor may hold itself out as having, any obligation to perform any activity or to enter into any business relationship in relation to the purpose for which confidential information was made available. Neither party may make any public announcement or press release c oncerning the purpose or this agreement without the prior written consent of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Parties to whom the information is imparted must be warned that include d ‘insider information ,’ regarding publicly listed and traded shares may preclud e them from such trade in those shares . The parties are independent parties and nothing in this agreement constitutes a joint venture, partnership or agency. Neither party has, nor may hold itself out as having, any obligation to perform any activity or to enter into any business relationship in relation to the purpose for which confidential information was made available. Neither party may make any public announcement or press release c oncerning the purpose or this agreement without the prior written consent of the other party.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the warning about insider trading to the beginning of the RELATIONSHIP OF PARTIES section makes it easy to overlook. The section focuses on the relationship of the parties involved and doesn't intuitively lead to a search for information regarding insider trading.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 1043A of the Corporations Act 2001 prohibits insider trading. Relocating the insider trading warning to the 'RELATIONSHIP OF PARTIES' clause makes it less visible, which reduces the clarity of the obligation not to trade on inside information, conflicting with legal requirements for adequate disclosure.

perturbation.justification: The change moves the insider trading warning to the 'RELATIONSHIP OF PARTIES' section. This is a structural flaw because this section does not intuitively lead to a search for information regarding insider trading. According to the law explanation, this reduces the clarity of the obligation not to trade on inside information. Section 1043A of the Corporations Act 2001 prohibits insider trading (as stated in the Law Explanation). Scraped Law Snippet 2 directly references Section 1043A, prohibiting individuals with inside information from trading or procuring others to trade. Moving the warning makes it less visible, which is a structural flaw that contradicts the legal requirement for adequate disclosure and could lead to violations of section 1043A.
